paper.title: Old, Metal-Poor Extreme Velocity Stars in the Solar Neighborhood

extraction.summary: 30 newly discovered extreme velocity stars (Gaia-T-ES1 through Gaia-T-ES30) from Gaia DR2 archive. Old, metal-poor giants (-1.5<[Fe/H]<-1.0) with v_total~480-600 km/s, located within ~8 kpc. Selected from 1743 high-v_tan stars with E>0 or E>-4840 km^2/s^2 in MWPotential2014. Paper argues they are probably bound, implying v_esc~600 km/s and M_200~1.4e12 Msun. 2 stars (Gaia-T-ES29, ES24) consistent with GC origin; 3 stars (Gaia-T-ES12, ES21, ES22) possibly from LMC. Gaia DR2 source_ids provided for all 30 stars.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2: selected stars with parallax_over_error>10, radial_velocity not null, v_tan>300 km/s. 1743 candidates.

source_refs.context: Sec 2: 1743 stars with parallax_over_error>10, v_tan>300 km/s

method_chain.summary: Compute orbital energy E in MWPotential2014. Select 30 stars with E>0 or E>-4840 km^2/s^2 (marginally bound). Sorted by decreasing E, named Gaia-T-ESi.

source_refs.context: Sec 2: 30 stars unbound or marginally bound in MWPotential2014

method_chain.summary: MWPotential2014 (Bovy 2015): NFW halo + Miyamoto-Nagai disk + power-law bulge. Solar: R0=8 kpc, (U,V,W)=(11.1,232.24,7.25) km/s, v0=220 km/s.
MC sampling (1000 draws) from error distributions accounting for correlations. Compute P_unb: fraction of samples with E>0. P_unb=0.44-1.00 for the 30 stars.
Backward orbit integration for 30 stars + LMC (test particle). 1000 orbits each. LMC: v_los=262.2±3.4 km/s, pm=(1.850±0.03,0.234±0.03) mas/yr.

source_refs.context: Sec 3.3: Orbit integration, 1000 samples, LMC orbit

method_chain.summary: Origin assessment: 18 stars cross disk at r<30 kpc. Gaia-T-ES29: P(r<0.25 kpc from GC)=0.55, flight time~9 Myr. Gaia-T-ES24: P(r<0.25 kpc)=0.08. Gaia-T-ES22: P(d_LMC<5 kpc)=0.27 at ~200 Myr ago.
Dust-corrected CMD with PARSEC isochrones: most are old (>1 Gyr), metal-poor (-1.5<[Fe/H]<-1.0) giants. RAVE DR5 + LAMOST DR3 metallicities for 7 stars confirm low metallicity [M/H]<=-0.9.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Gaia-T-ES1: Gaia DR2 3252546886080448384, l=193.87°, b=-36.61°, d_helio=1.18 ± 0.09 kpc, r=9.0 kpc, v_total=598.3 km/s, v_r=-337.0 km/s, v_los=1.7 km/s, P_unb=0.95. Old, metal-poor giant with extreme velocity. Origin unknown. Old, metal-poor halo giant.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly presents these 30 stars as 'extreme velocity stars' discovered in Gaia DR2. Table 1 provides full 6D phase space and Gaia source_ids. Paper argues they are probably bound (v_esc~600 km/s) but are unbound in MWPotential2014. MC sampling of errors included.
